sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports elbow pain for approximately four days, primarily in the inside of his elbow. He states the pain is exacerbated by activities and he has tried ibuprofen with limited relief. He denies any trauma or prior injuries to the elbow. He reports that the pain keeps him from lifting and doing things sometimes.
medical_concepts: limited range of motion with lifting

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals moderate tenderness to palpation of the medial epicondyle. Pain is elicited with resisted wrist flexion and pronation. Vital signs are within normal limits. The x-ray shows no fracture or bony abnormality.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with medial epicondylitis, likely secondary to overuse from pottery activities.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am ordering a sling to be worn at all times. I am recommending ice packs to the elbow for 20 minutes, three times daily. I am ordering ibuprofen 600 mg every 6 hours with food for one week. I will schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to assess progress.

medical_concepts: limited range of motion with lifting
